title: Savings vs Borrowing
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: In what situations would you prefer to use savings instead of credit in your own life?
What kind of emergencies or goals are you currently NOT saving for but would benefit from a small savings buffer?

stages.prompt: You have ₹8,000 in savings and face a one‑time emergency expense of ₹5,000. What is usually the wiser choice?

options.label: Use savings and then slowly rebuild them
options.reflection: Correct. Savings are meant to protect you from needing high‑cost credit in such emergencies.

options.label: Take a high‑interest loan to keep savings untouched
options.reflection: Keeping savings but taking an expensive loan often increases your total stress and repayment burden.

options.label: Ignore the emergency and delay payment
options.reflection: Delaying an important emergency payment can create bigger health, job, or family problems later.

options.label: Take small loans from many places to feel safer
options.reflection: Many small loans quickly become confusing and risky. A clear plan with savings is usually safer.

stages.prompt: Why is building even a small emergency savings fund helpful?

options.label: It is not helpful; credit will always be available
options.reflection: Credit is not guaranteed and can be very costly. Savings give you more control and choice.

options.label: It reduces dependence on loans for every shock
options.reflection: Yes. Even a small buffer can prevent you from entering a debt cycle for minor emergencies.

options.label: It is mainly to show others that you have money
options.reflection: Savings are for your stability and peace of mind, not for show.

options.label: It encourages you to spend more because money is available
options.reflection: Healthy savings habits come with discipline. The goal is to use them wisely, not to spend faster.
